name: bt-aux-fix
description: PAUSED + CARPLAY WARNING - do NOT install this if you run an aftermarket CarPlay adapter (GTA CarKits, and likely Ottocast / CarlinKit): those feed CarPlay audio into the PCM's wired AUX input and require AUX enabled with AUX-Bluetooth DISABLED. Steering the source toward BT/A2DP makes the phone bond to the PCM's native Bluetooth for media, which blocks wireless CarPlay's BT->WiFi handoff, so the CarPlay handshake stalls and never connects (confirmed in the field on a 958 Cayenne with a GTA kit). If CarPlay already broke: turn AUX-Bluetooth OFF in the PCM (insert a CD -> DISC -> OPTION -> SET AUX), forget the PCM pairing on the phone, then re-pair only the adapter. Separately, the boot-source fix has been measured inert at boot: the byte it flips lives in the LastMode persistence handler, which PCM3Root never reaches at startup (the boot decision is made elsewhere and falls to FM before Bluetooth is up). So it does NOT reliably keep the unit on Bluetooth, and has been disabled to prevent accidental installs while a brick-safe replacement - a DSI source-switch that rides the phone-connect event - is in development. This entry now offers only 'Revert to stock': use it to cleanly remove a previously-applied fix and its /HBpersistence boot hook. Never touches the read-only flash. Builds on WillCoder's PCM_31_AUX-BT research.
author: dspl1236

values.label: Revert to stock - remove any previously-applied fix + boot hook
